Title: Plant perception of β-aminobutyric acid is mediated by an aspartyl-tRNA synthetase

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; NahG Col-0; Ler backgrounds used
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: L-aspartic acid; β-aminobutyric acid (BABA) perception-linked Asp homeostasis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BABA primes broad-spectrum disease resistance; Asp is central amino acid substrate in translation-related metabolism
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated cytoplasmic strands; pathogen/BABA-primed relocalization to peripheral cytoplasm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basally expressed; transcription induced by Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis infection; not induced by BABA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S cauliflower mosaic virus promoter (constitutive) used for IBI1:YFP overexpression/complementation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: R-BABA treatment; pathogen attack by H. arabidopsidis; Plectosphaerella cucumerina infection; rhizobacterium Pseudomonas fluorescens WCS417r; SA and ABA comparison treatments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for BABA-induced immunity and ISR; mediates BABA perception; promotes pathogen-induced defence priming while also influencing BABA-associated Asp accumulation and growth repression sensitivity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; allelism tests; T-DNA mutants; genetic complementation with 35S:IBI1:YFP; pathogen resistance assays; callose assays; PR1 priming assays; subcellular localization; immunoprecipitation-mass spectrometry for BABA binding; Asp quantification
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: eIF2α (functional downstream phosphorylation marker); GCN2 (genetic downstream kinase in growth pathway)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IBI1 (At4g31180) was identified by map-based cloning as the gene mutated in ibi1 lines impaired in BABA-induced resistance. 2. IBI1 encodes an AspRS but also acts as the plant receptor/perceptor for the active R-enantiomer of BABA in planta. 3. Pathogen attack induces IBI1 expression and BABA primes its relocalization within the cytoplasmic domain, consistent with a non-canonical defence role. 4. Loss of IBI1 abolishes BABA-induced priming of callose and PR1 responses and blocks rhizobacteria-induced ISR. 5. IBI1 also contributes to BABA-induced Asp accumulation and growth repression sensitivity, but immunity and growth effects separate genetically downstream of GCN2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to uncouple broad-spectrum BABA-induced disease resistance from growth penalty; engineering IBI1 expression or BABA-perception properties may improve crop defence priming
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although IBI1 is an enzyme, the paper’s core emphasis is its non-canonical receptor/perception role rather than only catalytic pathway function; therefore classified here as Common Gene rather than Pathway Gene. Overexpression increased basal resistance even without BABA.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: L-aspartic acid / uncharged tRNAAsp-associated amino acid stress response
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediator of amino acid stress-linked translational repression and growth inhibition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes BABA-induced growth repression but is dispensable for BABA-induced immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (gcn2-1); eIF2α phosphorylation immunoblot; growth repression assay; disease resistance assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BABA induces eIF2α phosphorylation in a GCN2-dependent manner. 2. gcn2-1 relieves BABA-induced growth repression. 3. gcn2-1 does not impair BABA-induced resistance, demonstrating pathway separation between growth repression and immunity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point to reduce BABA-associated growth penalty without compromising induced resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a metabolite biosynthetic gene or transcriptional regulator; included as a core experimentally validated signaling component in amino acid stress output.
